entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:
Hello Graziela,

After applying TR Phase I and notes through SP-Level 209 for Release 604, the Tax Form Group text in T51T1 for Box 14 Tax Groups W24A through W24J were overlaid.  The description of the text we had configured before is now generalized text Box 14 amount 1 through Box 14 amount 10.  This has not been experienced in previous years.  The text has always remained as configured and labeled per our customer requirement.  Is this a normal behavior for the text to be overlaid with current Tax Reporter notes?  

Our intent is to relabel back to how we had before unless you have an alternate suggestion or advise against doing otherwise.

Kind Regards

Debbie

entry.title: De activate (unhide) UI.HIDE feature from standard CDS view
entry.content:
In C_SUPPLIER CDS view we have BANCKIDENTIFICATION field it have has UI.HIDE annotation is active because of this we can see BANCKIDENTIFICATION field in CDS view level but not at "Display Supplier list" app level. business requirement to show this field in APP. 

Please guide me how we can show unhide or show  BANCKIDENTIFICATION field at app level.

entry.title: NexGen AI Agents for SAP S4 HANA
entry.content:
NVIDIA’s Jensen Huang says that - 'IT will become the HR of AI agents'. Source

Epoch

Creating AI agents begins with defining their purpose and value. Managing their lifecycle—from development to deployment—can be complex and challenging. When it comes to SAP S/4HANA, finding a ready-made solution for AI agents is rare. However, thanks to NexGen.AI, we now have SAP AI Agents that can address some of these challenges. In this blog series, I’ll share how I built an AI agent group in 2024 that sped up SAP processes and significantly reduced manual effort.

 

The Challenge 

One of the biggest hurdles we encountered daily was dealing with unstructured emails. Automating this process wasn’t straightforward because:

  1. Emails had to be monitored, categorized, and forwarded to the right team.
  2. Team members manually extracted information from emails to process them further.
  3. This created a heavy workload, particularly for email categorization and processing file attachments.
  4. Additionally, each email needed to be acknowledged with an appropriate response.

To address these issues, we identified the need for advanced automation in pre-SAP processes. Unfortunately, the SAP ecosystem offers limited solutions that integrate SAP-specific knowledge with automation capabilities.

By leveraging NexGen AI Agents and SAP Build Process automations, we developed a solution to handle incoming emails intelligently. This AI agent could automatically sort emails, extract key information, process attachments, and send accurate responses—all with minimal human intervention.

This project demonstrated how AI agents can streamline and accelerate SAP processes, saving time and reducing effort while improving overall efficiency.

 

Is the AI-Agent already here from the Movie 'Matrix'?  

The moment has arrived, and we’ve entered a quiet tussle with AI. As humans, we just can’t resist filling up empty spaces—if we see one, we’re all over it. Remember that one person who hilariously claimed ownership of the Moon? Well, I spotted a free space in AI agent integrations with SAP business processes—and, of course, I couldn’t resist jumping in to make the most of it!

Working with AI agents is a bit like having a pet dog you adore. At first, the dog is clueless about life’s purpose but is very clear about its love for food—and you love it despite its chaos. But as any dog owner knows, with daily training and patience, even the most scatterbrained pup can learn new tricks.

AI agents are no different. Start them off with small, simple tasks so they can learn quickly and perform better. By breaking down big problems into bite-sized pieces, we can train these “AI puppies” to become specialized pros, equipped with the right tools to nail every task. Bonus: no chewed-up shoes!

Now, here’s how we’ve planned to show off these AI agents and their superpowers in automating business processes like pros!

The image above provides an overview of the high-level process flow and scope. The AI agents I have used or prepared are out-of-the-box (OOB) solutions from different platforms.

Application Scopes

The model integrates with:

  • Outlook
  • Azure
  • SAP S/4HANA
  • ServiceNow

Agentic Tools in Scope

The AI agent functionalities are built using:

  • Azure Logic App
  • SAP Build 
  • LangChain Framework
  • SAP AI Core - SAP Generative AI Hub

Overview of AI Agents

The process begins with an inbound email received at your business email address.

AI Agent: Email Analyzer

This agent leverages Cognitive AI and fine-tuned large language models to analyze emails and extract key details, such as:

  • Email Intent
  • Details of Email Attachments
  • OCR Processing for Attachments

AI Agent: Business Analyst

This agent is a collection of multiple mini-agents equipped with specialized tools to perform a variety of tasks. In this scenario, the tools include:

  • SAP OData AI: Extracts user-requested information and converts it into API calls.
  • SAP Workflow Tool: Creates workflows in SAP based on the inbound email.
  • ServiceNow Integration: Directly interacts with ServiceNow APIs to extract information and provide responses.
  • RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation): Connects to a knowledge base to handle user queries effectively.

AI Agent: Email Responder

This agent uses Generative AI to craft email responses. After processing inputs from previous agents, it generates a reply that is both accurate and context-aware. With the right prompts, it achieves results that surpass traditional solutions like ChatGPT, providing seamless and efficient communication.

This streamlined approach ensures end-to-end automation for business processes, making them faster and more efficient. 

Now, let’s dive into these two key components (and sprinkle in a bit of humor):

Shared Memory

This is where the magic happens. Shared memory is like the group chat for all the agents—except it’s actually productive. Think of it as the baton in a relay race: when one agent finishes its leg, it hands over the processed info to the next agent, saying, “Your turn, buddy!” With an organized shared memory template, the whole system runs smoother than a well-trained pet dog (you know, the one that finally stops chewing your shoes and starts fetching them).

Processing Logs

Now, logs might sound boring, but trust me, they’re the unsung heroes here. Imagine having a diary where every decision the LLM agent makes is recorded—complete with timestamps and all the juicy details. The best way to do this? Build a CAP application and store logs periodically. Not only does this give you confidence (“Hey, we’ve got receipts for that decision!”), but it also saves you when things go sideways. If the AI messes up, you’ve got a central “oops archive” to figure out what went wrong and fix it.

Revisiting the Steps:

1. New Email Arrival
A new email lands in your Outlook or Gmail inbox, triggering the event listener. This fires off a chain reaction to kickstart the process.

2. Email Analyzer Agent
The first stop in the chain is the Email Analyzer agent. It extracts all the relevant details from the email—like intent, attachments, or critical data—and passes this information to the next agent, the Business Analyst.

3. Business Analyst Agent
This is the MVP of the process. The Business Analyst agent takes the extracted information (a mix of structured and semi-structured input) and applies a “Chain of Thought” approach to generate outputs tailored to the input. Whether it’s interacting with your ERP systems like SAP S/4HANA or ECC, or connecting with ServiceNow and similar tools, this agent gets the job done.

4. Email Responder Agent
Finally, we reach the closing act. The Email Responder agent steps in to craft a polished response, ensuring the process ends with a well-written reply.

 

Is this the End of B2B APIs?

AI agents and event-driven systems aren’t replacing B2B APIs—they’re changing how we use them. APIs are essential for securely sharing data between systems, but their role is evolving. Instead of just being fixed tools for specific tasks, APIs now support smarter, AI-driven processes. Think of APIs as the foundation, while AI agents bring flexibility and intelligence. This shift doesn’t make APIs less important—it makes them more powerful and adaptable for today’s business needs.

What about this Business Analyst Agent?

Haha! AI agents can be a bit chaotic at times. As a real Business Analyst, it’s your job to keep an eye on them. These agents often struggle to figure out the next steps or process certain data correctly. Your role is to guide and channel their efforts to make them work effectively. The good news? These AI agents are here to handle the blunt, repetitive work, freeing you up to focus on solving critical business problems. While their responses are around 50–60% accurate, they’re a helpful starting point with your expertise leading the way!

 

feel free to share your thought..

entry.title: Re: Sap sd
entry.content:
Dear Shubham,

You can add batch or field CHARG in your condition table to meet the requirement.

Regards

Sahil

entry.title: Tcode: CKMLCP : Error Material XX Curr. type 00: Cost component sum 1,XXX.XX differs from 5XX.XX
entry.content:
Hi SAP Expert,

Please help to check the issue for CO Module 

Tcode: CKMLCP : Error Material XX Curr. type 00: Cost component sum 1,XXX.XX differs from 5XX.XX

entry.title: Sales Order as Custom Fields do not work
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I have a request to add Sales Order from Account Assignment of Purchase Requisition Items to the screen Process Purchase Requisition (V2).

I have created a custom field named YY1_SALESORDERPURRQ with business context Procurement: Purchase Requisition Item, then I created custom logic using extension point Change of Purchase Requisition Item.

May I ask if there is any problem with my code in custom logic because it doesn't do anything at all.

IF purchaserequisitionitem-YY1_SALESORDERIDEXTPRQ_PRI IS INITIAL AND
purchaserequisitionitem-YY1_SALESORDERPURRQ_PRI IS INITIAL.
    LOOP AT PURCHASEREQACCASSGNMT_TABLE INTO DATA(prqaccountassgnmt).
        IF prqaccountassgnmt-SALESDOCUMENT IS NOT INITIAL.
            purchaserequisitionitemchange-YY1_SALESORDERPURRQ_PRI = prqaccountassgnmt-SALESDOCUMENT.
        ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
ENDIF.

entry.title: Hana 2.0 SP5 on SUSE Linux 15 Sp3 - support end in 31 dec 2025
entry.content:
Hi Team , 

We have HANA 2.0 sp5 running on  SUSE Linux 15 Sp3, the Linux support is ending on 31st Dec, 2025.

  1. What is the latest SUSE OS version recommended for HANA 2.00.057.
  2. How to upgrade SUSE OS ? 
  3. What are the impacts of not going for upgrade even after 31 Dec DIGITS.

Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Hana 2.0 SP5 on SUSE Linux 15 Sp3 - support end in 31...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Thank you for contacting SAP Support.

Regarding to your 1st concern, could you please refer to SAP KBA 2235581 - SAP HANA: Supported Operating Systems ?
You'll be able to see that SLES 15 SP3 is the latest supporting OS version for the current HANA revision.

So, if you have a plan to upgrade OS version, then you should first upgrade HANA database to the latest SPS05(2.00.059.16) or HANA 2.0 SPS07, SPS08.
Please be informed that HANA 2.0 SPS05 will also be ended of mainstream maintenance at Dec 31, 2025.
As for this, you can take a look at SAP Note 2378962 - SAP HANA 2.0 Revision and Maintenance Strategy.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Agentic AI in SAP: Revolutionizing Business Processes
entry.content:
Introduction:

In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape, businesses across industries are seeking innovative solutions to streamline operations and enhance decision-making. One such groundbreaking technology is Agentic AI, a form of artificial intelligence designed to act autonomously and drive business value. When integrated with SAP systems, Agentic AI can transform enterprise operations by automating complex processes, reducing human intervention, and improving accuracy.

This blog explores how Agentic AI is reshaping SAP business processes, enhancing efficiency, and delivering unprecedented value.

Understanding Agentic AI

Agentic AI refers to intelligent systems that possess agency—the ability to perceive their environment, make decisions, and act independently to achieve specific goals. Unlike traditional automation, which follows predefined rules and scripts, Agentic AI leverages machine learning, natural language processing, and advanced analytics to:

  • Learn from historical data.
  • Predict outcomes.
  • Take proactive actions.

For enterprises running SAP, this capability opens doors to self-driven operations, significantly reducing manual intervention and operational errors.

Applications of Agentic AI in SAP

Agentic AI can revolutionize various aspects of SAP-driven enterprises by introducing a new level of automation and intelligence:

1. Intelligent Financial Operations:

  • Automating accounts payable and receivable processes.
  • Detecting anomalies in financial transactions.
  • Predicting cash flow and optimizing working capital.

2. Supply Chain Optimization:

  • Forecasting demand and automating inventory management.
  • Optimizing logistics and transportation routes in real-time.
  • Identifying supply chain risks and suggesting mitigations.

3. Human Resources Management:

  • Enhancing talent acquisition by screening resumes and predicting candidate success.
  • Automating employee engagement surveys and providing actionable insights.
  • Streamlining payroll and benefits administration.

4. Sales and Customer Relationship Management:

  • Predicting customer needs and personalizing sales strategies.
  • Automating lead generation and qualification processes.
  • Enhancing customer support with AI-powered chatbots.

5. Production and Manufacturing:

  • Monitoring equipment health and predicting maintenance needs.
  • Optimizing production schedules to minimize downtime.
  • Enhancing quality control with AI-driven defect detection.

Benefits of Agentic AI in SAP

Integrating Agentic AI into SAP systems offers numerous advantages:

  1. Increased Efficiency: Automation of routine tasks allows employees to focus on strategic initiatives, enhancing overall productivity.
  2. Improved Decision-Making: AI-driven insights enable informed decision-making, reducing reliance on intuition and guesswork.
  3. Enhanced Accuracy: By minimizing human intervention, Agentic AI reduces the likelihood of errors in critical business processes.
  4. Scalability: AI-driven processes can scale effortlessly, accommodating business growth without a proportional increase in operational costs.
  5. Cost Savings: By optimizing resource utilization and reducing manual effort, businesses can achieve significant cost savings.

Challenges and Considerations

While the benefits of Agentic AI are compelling, businesses must address certain challenges to maximize its potential:

  1. Data Quality: AI systems require high-quality data for accurate predictions. Organizations must invest in data cleansing and management.
  2. Change Management: Adopting AI-driven processes may face resistance from employees. Effective change management strategies are crucial to ensure smooth adoption.
  3. Integration Complexity: Seamless integration of Agentic AI with existing SAP systems requires careful planning and execution.
  4. Ethical Concerns: Ensuring data privacy and addressing ethical implications of AI-driven decisions are vital.

The Future of Agentic AI in SAP

The adoption of Agentic AI in SAP is still in its early stages, but the potential is immense. Future advancements in AI technology will further enhance its capabilities, enabling:

  • Hyper-Automation: Extending automation beyond repetitive tasks to more complex decision-making processes.
  • Real-Time Analytics: Providing instant insights for faster and more accurate decision-making.
  • Personalized User Experiences: Tailoring interfaces and workflows to individual user preferences and behaviors.

As SAP systems continue to evolve, Agentic AI will play a pivotal role in driving innovation and ensuring businesses stay competitive in a digital-first world.

KTern.AI: Driving Innovation in SAP Transformations

KTern.AI leverages Agentic AI to redefine SAP transformations, offering an AI-First Delivery approach that automates, optimizes, and accelerates digital transformation processes. Here’s a snapshot of its core capabilities:

  • Automated Assessments: KTern.AI automates SAP landscape assessments, providing actionable insights to optimize custom codes, business processes, and system configurations. These data-driven insights enable proactive optimization, ensuring strategic and efficient transformations.
  • Risk-Based Testing: KTern.AI prioritizes testing efforts based on risk, focusing on critical business processes while ensuring comprehensive coverage. Its generative AI-driven test case recommendations enhance efficiency, accelerating project timelines without compromising quality.

Conclusion

Agentic AI represents a paradigm shift in how businesses manage and optimize their operations. By enabling autonomous decision-making and action, it empowers organizations to achieve greater agility, efficiency, and resilience.
With KTern.AI’s innovative capabilities, businesses can harness the full potential of Agentic AI within their SAP ecosystems, paving the way for a future defined by intelligent automation and data-driven decision-making.
The journey to an AI-first enterprise begins today, and KTern.AI is your trusted partner in navigating this transformative landscape.

entry.title: SAP QM Result recording screen QE51N, with tool tip on result row or Short Text
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP QM Result recording screen QE51N, is there a way to show a tool tip on Short text like "Enter Quantitative value or Qualitative value" as required.

Regards

entry.title: PO outputs are sent to suppliers when attach any Attachment Via ME22N
entry.content: I am trying to attach an attachment through service objects icon, but this is sending the successful output message even though adding an attachment is not print relevant. Please help me understand why the output is getting sent successfully in this scenario.

entry.title: Unit of Measure related settings in SAP
entry.content:
Unit of Measure related settings in SAP

Purpose of document

This document is intended to serve as a guideline for creation of a new unit of measure and its related issues.
Introduction: Unit of Measure

Definition - Unit of Measure is the criterion based on which you measure the quantity of the material. Unit of measure is a value for a physical size.

Examples
Length dimension includes meter, centimeter and inch

Time dimension includes second, minute and hour

There are 4 different Unit of Measures used

Base Unit of Measure: This is the unit of measure in which the stocks of a material are managed. The system converts all quantities entered in other units to the base unit of measure. All material postings will occur with respect to base unit of measure.

Sales Unit of Measure: Allows a material to be sold in a unit differing from the base unit of measure. The sales unit is proposed automatically in the sales order, where it can be changed.

(Purchase)Ordering Unit of Measure: Allows a material to be purchase in a unit differing from the base unit of measure. The purchase order unit is proposed automatically in a purchase order, where it can be changed.

Unit of Issue: Unit of measure in which the material is issued from the warehouse. It allows consumption, stock transfers, transfer postings, and physical inventories to be recorded in a unit differing from the base unit of measure.
These UoM's are linked with each other for every material by conversation table which is explained below in section - IV-alternative unit of measure

 

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/mickaelquesnot_cuni-activity-7283016294755360771-v1Zz?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop

entry.title: No CDS View can retrieve data of Material group in Sales org 2 view
entry.content:
Below fields existed in sales org 2 view in MM01, Which CDS view can retrieve these data?

Material group 1:MVKE-MVGR1

Material group 2:MVKE-MVGR2

Material group 3:MVKE-MVGR3

Material group 4:MVKE-MVGR4

Material group 5:MVKE-MVGR5

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: No CDS View can retrieve data of Material group in Sa...
entry.content: If you  need a CDS view to retrieve the value of MVKE-MVGR related to a specific product/sales org./distr.channel , you can use I_ProductSalesDelivery . In APP Customer data browser, you can use 'product number'/ 'sales organization'/ 'Distribution channel' as filter to find corresponding product group.

entry.title: Re: MACRS Depreciation incorrect value
entry.content: Hi Santhosh, is this really a question on the SAPUI5 UI technology? If it is rather on a business application, you might want to adjust the assigned tags?

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Spaces and Pages now listed in the SAP Fiori apps library

Welcome to SAP world,

You can use the SAP free online course link https://open.sap.com/

Regards

SS

entry.title: Agentic AI in SAP: Revolutionizing Business Processes
entry.content:
Introduction:

In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape, businesses across industries are seeking innovative solutions to streamline operations and enhance decision-making. One such groundbreaking technology is Agentic AI, a form of artificial intelligence designed to act autonomously and drive business value. When integrated with SAP systems, Agentic AI can transform enterprise operations by automating complex processes, reducing human intervention, and improving accuracy.
This blog explores how Agentic AI is reshaping SAP business processes, enhancing efficiency, and delivering unprecedented value.

Understanding Agentic AI:

Agentic AI refers to intelligent systems that possess agency—the ability to perceive their environment, make decisions, and act independently to achieve specific goals. Unlike traditional automation, which follows predefined rules and scripts, Agentic AI leverages machine learning, natural language processing, and advanced analytics to:

  • Learn from historical data.
  • Predict outcomes.
  • Take proactive actions.

For enterprises running SAP, this capability opens doors to self-driven operations, significantly reducing manual intervention and operational errors.

Applications of Agentic AI in SAP

Agentic AI can revolutionize various aspects of SAP-driven enterprises by introducing a new level of automation and intelligence:

1. Intelligent Financial Operations:

  • Automating accounts payable and receivable processes.
  • Detecting anomalies in financial transactions.
  • Predicting cash flow and optimizing working capital.

2. Supply Chain Optimization:

  • Forecasting demand and automating inventory management.
  • Optimizing logistics and transportation routes in real-time.
  • Identifying supply chain risks and suggesting mitigations.

3. Human Resources Management:

  • Enhancing talent acquisition by screening resumes and predicting candidate success.
  • Automating employee engagement surveys and providing actionable insights.
  • Streamlining payroll and benefits administration.

4. Sales and Customer Relationship Management:

  • Predicting customer needs and personalizing sales strategies.
  • Automating lead generation and qualification processes.
  • Enhancing customer support with AI-powered chatbots.

5. Production and Manufacturing:

  • Monitoring equipment health and predicting maintenance needs.
  • Optimizing production schedules to minimize downtime.
  • Enhancing quality control with AI-driven defect detection.

Benefits of Agentic AI in SAP

Integrating Agentic AI into SAP systems offers numerous advantages:

  • Increased Efficiency: Automation of routine tasks allows employees to focus on strategic initiatives, enhancing overall productivity.
  • Improved Decision-Making: AI-driven insights enable informed decision-making, reducing reliance on intuition and guesswork.
  • Enhanced Accuracy: By minimizing human intervention, Agentic AI reduces the likelihood of errors in critical business processes.
  • Scalability: AI-driven processes can scale effortlessly, accommodating business growth without a proportional increase in operational costs.
  • Cost Savings: By optimizing resource utilization and reducing manual effort, businesses can achieve significant cost savings.

Challenges and Considerations

While the benefits of Agentic AI are compelling, businesses must address certain challenges to maximize its potential:

  • Data Quality: AI systems require high-quality data for accurate predictions. Organizations must invest in data cleansing and management.
  • Change Management: Adopting AI-driven processes may face resistance from employees. Effective change management strategies are crucial to ensure smooth adoption.
  • Integration Complexity: Seamless integration of Agentic AI with existing SAP systems requires careful planning and execution.
  • Ethical Concerns: Ensuring data privacy and addressing ethical implications of AI-driven decisions are vital.

The Future of Agentic AI in SAP

The adoption of Agentic AI in SAP is still in its early stages, but the potential is immense. Future advancements in AI technology will further enhance its capabilities, enabling:

  • Hyper-Automation: Extending automation beyond repetitive tasks to more complex decision-making processes.
  • Real-Time Analytics: Providing instant insights for faster and more accurate decision-making.
  • Personalized User Experiences: Tailoring interfaces and workflows to individual user preferences and behaviors.

As SAP systems continue to evolve, Agentic AI will play a pivotal role in driving innovation and ensuring businesses stay competitive in a digital-first world.

KTern.AI: Driving Innovation in SAP Transformations:

KTern.AI leverages Agentic AI to redefine SAP transformations, offering an AI-First Delivery approach that automates, optimizes, and accelerates digital transformation processes. Here’s a snapshot of its core capabilities:

  • Automated Assessments: KTern.AI automates SAP landscape assessments, providing actionable insights to optimize custom codes, business processes, and system configurations. These data-driven insights enable proactive optimization, ensuring strategic and efficient transformations.
  • Risk-Based Testing: KTern.AI prioritizes testing efforts based on risk, focusing on critical business processes while ensuring comprehensive coverage. Its generative AI-driven test case recommendations enhance efficiency, accelerating project timelines without compromising quality.

Conclusion

Agentic AI represents a paradigm shift in how businesses manage and optimize their operations. By enabling autonomous decision-making and action, it empowers organizations to achieve greater agility, efficiency, and resilience.
With KTern.AI’s innovative capabilities, businesses can harness the full potential of Agentic AI within their SAP ecosystems, paving the way for a future defined by intelligent automation and data-driven decision-making.

entry.title: Re: Restrict cost center by GL account in Ariba
entry.content: yes relation entry is the typical solution to this requirement.

entry.title: Re: Inbound processing in endpoint at /EC_to_ERP_Master_D...
entry.content:
Dear Ivo,

 

these artifacts are on Neo, the versions are as follows and we’re utilizing web dispatcher not CC.

However, I have noticed a pattern that the Services are failing for every 3 calls, and the 4th attempt is succeeding and it’s repeating.

I submitted a ticket for SAP and there have been no updates so far.

  • Replicate Employee Master Data and Org. Assignments from SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central to SAP ERP or SAP S4HANA - Version: 8.1.0
  • Replicate Organizational Objects from SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central to SAP ERP or SAP S4HANA - Version: 13.0.0

entry.title: Change Delivery Batch No & quantity.
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

I want to change Outbound delivery Batch no & quantity.

Try BAPI : BAPI_OUTB_DELIVERY_CHANGE

Quantity change successfully with this BAPI for SAME batch ( Delivery batch = BAPI input batch )

but when I was change BAPI input batch then system given error.

 

Pradip Patel.

entry.title: Re: Consumption/Adjusting the usage Cycle to PRT in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Greetings,

how are you posting confirmations on your production order? Is it per operation or per header? Is in a single confirmation of you post multiple partial confirmations?

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Staying certified without LearningHub subscription?
entry.content:
SAP revamped their certification process about a year ago, introducing the requirement to renew certifications annually through a "Stay Certified" exam. At the time, I understood that once you purchased the certification, these renewal exams would be free.

Now, with the first renewal period approaching, I’ve discovered that you apparently need a Learning Hub subscription to even access the "Stay Certified" exam. Also Learning Hub page says there's another "Partner" version of it, but I cannot access the "Stay Certified" exam through the Partner Edge.

Am I misunderstanding something here, or are these renewal exams not actually free after all?

entry.title: Re: sap.m.radiobutton circle not visible
entry.content:
We have resolved the issue be adding Visibility in the standard CSS class for the radio button.

Thank you for your help.

entry.title: Object reference not set to instance of an object error when previewing Ageing Report
entry.content:
We are using SAP Business One HANA 10.0, While we are trying to preview "Detailed Receivables Aging Report" >> Business Partner Ageing (Details) - CR (GB) (System)
it gives error Object reference not set to instance of an object.

we are looking for the solution. thank you in advance.

entry.title: for billing type - G2 (credit memo) two different types of number range reflected
entry.content:
For billing type - G2 (credit memo) two different types of number range reflected. We need only one number range for credit memo. 

Kindly check below documents for reference,

1) Billing type - G2 (credit memo) - ST40000021 

2) Billing type - G2 (credit memo) - 90000207 

Why two different number range hitted while creating credit memo?

Need root cause for the same.

I am going to upload few transaction of Goods Receipt document and currently using the sheet for the table IGN1, here it showing in the first column showing "parent key" and the second is showing the "line number". 

Please let me know the meaning of this 2 columns

entry.title: Re: Inbound processing in endpoint at /EC_to_ERP_Master_Data_And_Org_Query failed
entry.content:
We're also experiencing the same issue since 11/01/2025, since the automatic SAP Cloud System Updates last weekend to their Cloud Integration environments.

We are running the Neo Environment on version 7.34.10 (with no Cloud Connector)

SAP Support Incident has been raised with the software vendor.

 

Error Details:
Inbound processing in endpoint at /EC_to_ERP_Generic_Object_Query failed with message "IllegalStateException:The request object has been recycled and is no longer associated with this facade"
 
SAP Cloud System Changes:
 

From: SAP CloudSupport Alerts <sapcloudsupport@alerts.ondemand.com>
Sent: Friday, January 10, 2025 11:12:10 PM
Subject: Monthly software update

SAP Cloud Integration

Customer Communication

Monthly software update

 

Dear Customer,

Customer Name: *********

Over the course of next two days, your tenant will be updated to the latest software version.
The update will be performed with zero downtime and hence will not impact your business.
For information on the latest feature delivery, kindly refer to our official documentation:

https://help.sap.com/viewer/368c481cd6954bdfa5d0435479fd4eaf/Cloud/en-US/72ef31d2739a4b079f0d297ffa684ec8.html

entry.title: Customising fiori applications by coding
entry.content:
So I want to write add a code in existing fiori app called "Manage assignements" wherein a button is to be desabled after reading a input screen and if the input screen matches a particular value. This code is working in the console in inspect tab at runtime. 

What we want to achive is to integrate this javascript code to the fiori app itself by default. Is it possible? What efforts are required?

 

(function() {
    function disableButtonIfConditionMet() {
        var inputElement = document.getElementById("application-DataSetMatchingAssignment-manage-component---worklist--smartFilterbar-filterItemControl_BASIC-DISP_GROUP-inner");
        var buttonElement = document.getElementById("application-DataSetMatchingAssignment-manage-component---worklist--btnAssign");
 
        if (inputElement && buttonElement) {
            if (inputElement.value === "1520 (Trading Partner balance)") {
                buttonElement.disabled = true;
            } else {
                buttonElement.disabled = false;
            }
        }
    }
 
    // Run the function initially
    disableButtonIfConditionMet();
 
    // Set an interval to run the function every second
    setInterval(disableButtonIfConditionMet, 1000);
})();

 

@SAPSupport SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA SAP Fiori Makers

entry.title: Re: for billing type - G2 (credit memo) two different typ...
entry.content:
Hello @servotechindia 

The system has determined two different number ranges to the same billing document type, likely due to flexible billing document number settings configured in the IMG Activity.

Please review SSCUI Activity: 103306 and refer to the attached screenshot for further details.

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Re: Restrict cost center by GL account in Ariba
entry.content: Thanks for confirmation

entry.title: Re: CAP - CDS search gives error "Search not possible bec...
entry.content:
You can avoid that the search is shown by adding:

@Capabilities.SearchRestrictions: {Searchable: false}

To the Entity from S/4HANA Cloud.

entry.title: Re: CREATE button is not appearing after deployed Fiori A...
entry.content: @saurabh__khare : I dont have option of New Page and i have only three options

entry.title: Bundle product in Subscription billing
entry.content:
Hi Team,
In our ongoing project we have integrated Subscription billing with SAP S4H public cloud.
We have a customer's requirement to setup bundle products in Subscription billing.
We don't have a scenario of solution order in our project.
Currently there are subscription products that can be sold individually or also as a part of bundle
Example:
Bundle product name : A1 Pack
items part of this bundle : RAM, License, OS

We have maintained above items as custom references in the product master in A1 Pack in Subscription billing. And A1 Pack has its price. 
thanks
SAP Subscription Billing

entry.title: Re: CREATE button is not appearing after deployed Fiori A...
entry.content: @saurabh__khare : The Three options for Creation Mode is inline,Creation Rows and creationRowsHiddeninEditMode and if select inline option and Create Button is appearing but when click on Create button it is not navigating to New Page and when select the creationRowsHiddeninEditMode and Create Button is appearing and when i click on CREATE button it is not navigating to New Page and i dont have option of New Page in CREATE mode

entry.title: Error while performing Inventory trasnfer ( warehouse transfer ) in SAP B1
entry.content:
Hello Everyone ,


Need guidance in this , while performing stock transfer ( warehouse transfer ) from SAP B1 , HANA environment , system is generating this error " 1). [SAP AG][LIBODBCHDB DLL][HDBODBC] Syntax error or access violation;257 sql syntax error: incorrect syntax near ",": line 1 col 8 '' (NFN1) (at pos 8)" after selecting the update button.

Need help to troubleshoot

entry.title: Badge display
entry.content: Not able to see my badge after successfully completing a mission. Kindly help!!!

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting Data Audit
entry.content: thanks for your sharing!!

entry.title: Standard Cost recalculate during invoice
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a pricing condition with % for calculating the price of Material as Cost + Markup.
The issue which we have is, those Orders created during year end mostly remains open until next year.
In the new year we have to run Cost roll, so the standard cost will get changed. When Standard cost changed, those open Orders while invoicing the Cost+Markup price get changed since SAP billing always recalculating the this Cost+Markup %. We set Copy control for Item Category as D (Copy pricing elements unchanged), but still it recalculate the value during billing. Any idea this can be fixed using standard config or we must go for enhancement.

entry.title: Re: Goals Rating through hyperlink in VarPay form
entry.content: Thank you for the feedback Xavier

entry.title: Ppm item closing is resulting in error
entry.content: Hi is it possible to close ppm item upon closing a ps project in standard? and when I'm trying to close an ppm item manually aswell it is resulting in error like" error in data handling object" can you please help?

entry.title: Can the field Location be selected correctly?
entry.content:
Hi Community Expert,

Can the field Locatin be selected correctly? or only SAP successfactors related location can be selected? I want to choose SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, but it failed....

entry.title: Re: Upload in app Post outgoing payment
entry.content: Hello, To assist you better, could you please provide more details about your question? For example, are you trying to upload multiple documents at a particular step? If you're referring to uploading multiple attachment files, you can do so by selecting the “Upload” button in the “Attachments” section to add multiple files at one go. Any specific information or context you can provide will be very helpful. Looking forward to your response! Thanks and Best regards, Anne

entry.title: Issue in processing multiple PO in behavior class using MODIFY EML statement
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have a requirement to create new entries in existing PO Item-> Pricing Element where the values are passed from a behavior implementation class of a custom table in a loop.

MODIFY EML update statement (to create NEW entries inside a particular po item) works only for last PO in the loop, but not the earlier PO's passed from the loop. Note: COMMIT statement not allowed in a behavior class. I too tried with just hardcoded values outside any loop, by executing MODIFY EML statement twice for different POs one by one. Here too, only the last hardcoded unique PO->PO item-> pricing elements were created in the system.

How to make all POs inserted with new pricing elements when different POs are passed dynamically?

MODIFY EML statement:

MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_purchaseordertp_2 PRIVILEGED

ENTITY purchaseorderitem

CREATE BY \_purordpricingelement SET FIELDS WITH VALUE #(

( %key-purchaseorder = 'PO1'

%key-purchaseorderitem = '0010'

%target = VALUE #( (

%cid = |CID{ sy-tabix }|

conditiontype = 'FVA1'

conditionrateamount = '1'

conditioncurrency = 'USD' ) ) ) )

MAPPED DATA(mapped_pe1)

FAILED DATA(failed_pe1)

REPORTED DATA(reported_pe1).

 

MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_purchaseordertp_2 PRIVILEGED

ENTITY purchaseorderitem

CREATE BY \_purordpricingelement SET FIELDS WITH VALUE #(

( %key-purchaseorder = 'PO2'

%key-purchaseorderitem = '0010'

%target = VALUE #( (

%cid = |CID{ sy-tabix }|

conditiontype = 'FVA1'

conditionrateamount = '2'

conditioncurrency = 'USD' ) ) ) )

MAPPED DATA(mapped_pe2)

FAILED DATA(failed_pe2)

REPORTED DATA(reported_pe2).

 

In the above code, only the last PO - 'PO2' is getting executed by creating new entries of pricing element in the 'PO2', Line item '0010'.

entry.title: Polish local finance requirement - tax authority related
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

According to our research, there is a complex interaction between value-added tax (VAT) and income tax in Poland . When calculating income tax, Polish companies must adhere to detailed regulations regarding the deductibility of various costs, such as interest expenses and foreign exchange gains and losses. The tax authorities require that the costs related to taxable and non-taxable activities (KUP/NKUP) be clearly distinguished in the accounting system and that this information be accurately reported in monthly tax returns.

We need your assistance to confirm whether there is holistic standard solution can fullfil the local regulations both in booking and reporting in SAP S/4HANA Public Edition?

Appreciate your feedback!

Br, Bob

entry.title: Re: Publish RFQ from custom behavior class save method
entry.content:
Hello All,

Request you to close this thread i got the solution. we need to pass cookies along with csrf token will help resolve the issue and make successful API call.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Is it Possible to create a column in Variable Pay tha...
entry.content: What I would do is turn these columns from your screenshot into Amount so that there is never any sign in front of it, then I would add a custom column next to the business goal name with a lookup table with attribute = busgoalname where I add in plain text whether the values that come next are Percentages or Money. @juanantonioga

entry.title: Re: Parallel operations in IBP S&OP TS Based Heuristics
entry.content:
Hi Carsten,
Thank you for the answer.
Just to be clear : the requirement is if there are 4 different operations to be performed one after the other in sequence
Operation 1 -> Operation 2 -> Operation 3 -> Operation 4 
Each operation takes 1 week. So , if the demand is 1000 in Week 8, then each operation should be scheduled one week prior to each other.
Operation 1 done in Week 4; Operation 2 in Week 5; Operation 3 done in Week 6; Operation 4 done in Week 7. 
So final available receipts at end of Week 8 for the FG = 1000.
Attaching snapshot for your reference.
Can you please suggest if this can be achieved in Time series based supply planning.

entry.title: Re: accounts receivable and advance accounts of the same...
entry.content: 1.The key value of advance receipt account allocation is order, but the allocation field in the account is not displayed.

entry.title: Unrealized forex revaluation gain to only post to IFRS ledger and Not Local GAAP in Morocco
entry.content:
Dear Experts

In Morocco, The legal requirements states that unrealized forex revaluation gain should not be posted to Local GAAP ledger but unrealized forex revaluation loss should be posted to local GAAP. My client uses two ledgers IFRS and Local GAAP.

My question is, how can we allow posting of unrealized forex revaluation gain to only go to IFRS Ledger and not local GAAP. But for unrealized loss to go to both ledgers?

entry.title: Re: How to update a RAP object within another RAP-object?
entry.content:
Hi, 

like yunwu also replied.

The commit should be done by the framework automaticly. I've a similar scenario where I modify entities of a different BO within the RAP LUW. But this is done inside the action implementation ( and not inside the save_modified ) ans this is working well for me

entry.title: Re: accounts receivable and advance accounts of the same...
entry.content: 1.The key value of advance receipt account allocation is order, but the allocation field in the account is not displayed.2.When filling in the same allocation field manually, accounts receivable and advance receipts cannot be automatically liquidated；3.Can the unequal amounts of accounts receivable and accounts received in advance be liquidated?

entry.title: Re: Sap sd
entry.content:
Hello @Subham15 

Could you please clarify your business requirements? Typically, in the sales order process, batch determination is not performed due to certain batch limitations. Instead, the batch is determined during the outbound delivery stage.

You can meet your requirement by adding a new access sequence for the pricing condition, as highlighted by @Sahil_Grover .

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Re: Define Statutory Report layout not enabled (F2131)
entry.content:
Hi,

Pl refer the below note.

3545256 - DRC- Access to Define Statutory Reports (F2131) - SAP for Me

Regards

Parthasarathy

entry.title: Re: Problem updating delivery date (EINDT) and statistical delivery date (SLFDT) in PO badi
entry.content:
@Ryan-CrosbyThanks a lot for your time. Yes i tried it already past week but just to be sure i tried it again this morning, during the second call of PROCESS_SCHEDULE, data that i filled during previous call are partially lost (or redetermined by the standard). Same behaviour with GET_PREVIOUS_DATA and GET_PRESISTENT_DATA.

It's when i force them like 2 times (for one item modified) that during the third process, data are saved. But when there are more items processes/changed, i have to adjust more times (i did it in debug)..

entry.title: Re: How to update a RAP object within another RAP-object?
entry.content: From what I have learned (but had no chance yet to try it out on my own) saving other BOs is not supposed to be done in the " save_modified" (don't know what else that method, implementing "with additional save" is supposed to be for, if someone knows, please share your knowledge). It should rather be done "at the end of derivations" or "at the end of validations" or some other point earlier in the process. I actually don't know how to do that in a long-term stable way because editing another BO has nothing to do with deriving or validating user-inputs. This is bound to break in a very nasty way, but it's the only way to make things work that I could find out up to now..

Thank you

Regards

entry.title: Re: Budget Control in Professional service in sap public...
entry.content:
Hi @gunadharshini 

Currently in S/4HANA public cloud  budget control for CPM projects feature is not available.

You to create a request in the customer Influence portal or through the Product Management Group.Our product management team would plan the enhancement in future release based on priority and complexity of the feature request
Kindly raise a feature request using the below KBA note.
2716534 - Request for new features in SAP S/4HANA Professional Services

Regards,
Monisha.

entry.title: Re: Count User Daily Activity
entry.content: Since all of those are postable documents, maybe simply use journal entry table OJDT? You can use TransType to determine which document type created journal entry, and UserSign for the user who created it.

entry.title: Re: How to update a RAP object within another RAP-object?
entry.content: Actually Business Events are not that "asynchronous" as one might think. From my experience if in save_modified an Business-Event is raised then in the handlers to that event any Commit will also crash, though I can't imagine why SAP would build it like that ... feels like a kernel bug to me.

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud : Pricing in service Contract
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am currently working with the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud edition. As part of a business requirement, I am creating a service contract using the "Manage Service Contract" app with the item category set as "Service Contract Item."

Could someone please guide me on how pricing is determined in the service contract? Additionally, which app should be used to maintain the pricing condition records for this scenario?

Thank you in advance for your help!

Also lookedup via forums and usually they say the format should be consistent on all rows.

My cells were defined as text fields, allow multiple lines, limit length to visible area, user entered optional and the binding is correct (internal table-field).

What did I miss? Please see my screenshot below.

 

If you encountered this, please help and provide some suggestions or comments.

Thanks,

Newcloudguy

entry.title: Re: Plan cost not flow in EPPM in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Publi...
entry.content:
Hi @Deepanshu9892 

Please refer to the below KBA for this issue
3453098 - Plan costs, budget, actual costs not displayed in project reports

Regards,
Monisha.

entry.title: Custom Movement Types in Inventory Management Fiori apps
entry.content:
Introduction

Custom movement types in Inventory Management - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - are variations of standard movement types that have been tailored to meet specific business requirements. These custom movement types can be identified by their initial character because they always start with 9, X, Y, or Z.

Creating a custom movement type is useful when you need more detailed specifications for a particular business process. The most straightforward method to create a custom movement type is to copy an existing standard movement type and then modify it according to your specific needs.

Custom movement types are currently only supported for Fiori apps Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document (F0843), Post Goods Receipt for Inbound Delivery (F2502) and Post Goods Receipt for Production Order (F3110), Post Goods Receipt for Process Order (F3244) and Post Subsequent Adjustment (F5476) via Cloud BAdI MIM_GR4XY_PROVIDE_DATA.

Note: This functionality only supports the main items of subcontracting purchase orders. Movement types for sub-items in subcontracting purchase orders cannot be modified.

Example

For example, let's say that a company needs to use movement type 101 -post goods receipt - but wants to post the goods receipt to a different account. To achieve this, they must first copy movement type 101. The following shows you how:

  1. In your configuration environment, find and open the configuration activity Copy of Movement Type (ID 103898).
  2. Select the row for movement type 101.
  3. Choose "Copy as" in the top toolbar. A new screen will open.
  4. In the Movement Type field, enter a new 3-character key of your choice. It must begin with 9, X, Y, or Z. For this example, we will use Z01.
  5. Choose Apply.
  6. To add a new text description for your new movement type, choose the row for the new movement type Z01.
  7. In the Dialog Structure, double-click on Short Texts.
  8. Choose Position.
  9. In the pop up, using an example of a company using English, enter the following: Language: EN, Movement Type: 901
  10. Choose Continue.
  11. Under Movement Type Text, enter a new description for your new movement type and press enter.
  12. Choose Save.
  13. To change the Help Texts for your new custom movement type, double-click Help Texts in the Dialog Structure.
  14. Enter your new texts into the Text column. Note: You must maintain the Help Texts for transaction code MIGO and, if available, MB11.
  15. Choose Save.

Note: If you create a custom movement type, we recommend creating a corresponding reversal movement type immediately. You can use the configuration activity Set Reversal Movement Types (ID 104062).

 

Custom Movement Types for Fiori apps

After creating your custom movement type, you must implement the BAdI MMIM_GR4XY_PROVIDE_DATA to enable the use of your new movement type in one of the supported Goods Receipt Fiori apps.

It is also possible to modify the control field logic in connection with the copied movement type (see example below). For more information about this BAdI, refer to App Extensibility: Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document.

All Goods Receipt Fiori apps support custom movement types using the above-mentioned BAdI, except for the Post Goods Receipt without Reference app. You can also implement the BAdI MMIM_GR4XY_CHECK_DATA to apply custom validation logic for the copied movement type before posting the material document.

The primary use case for copying a movement type is to enable customers to modify account determination when posting a goods receipt. However, it is also possible to implement different control field behavior for the copied movement type compared to the original, supported by the above-mentioned BAdI.

Example: Posting a Goods Receipt with a Reason Code

  • Original Movement Type 101: Does not require a reason code entry (field is suppressed).
  • Copied Movement Type Z01: Configured to require a reason code entry.

Cases:

  1. Without Control Field Logic Changes: The reason code entry remains suppressed when posting a goods receipt with a Fiori app.
  2. With Control Field Logic Changes: Implementing changes for the copied movement type supports posting a goods receipt using Z01 in the Fiori app, including the required reason code entry.

entry.title: Re: Error while performing Inventory trasnfer ( warehouse...
entry.content:
Hi,

Check on COPY of the database, by commenting/removing the additional code added to the SP_TransactionNotification Code or SP_PostTransactionNotifce.

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for Windows Keyboard delay
entry.content: Unfortunately, we were unable to detect any abnormalities in relation to the CPU or memory or other programs

entry.title: Re: BW Datasouce : Is it need to clean SMQ1 and RSA7 befo...
entry.content:
Hi Charlie
LBWQ (PreQueue) und RSA7 (BW Queue) need to be empty to re-initialize LO 2LIS DataSource.
Unless there will be "old" delta records in future, which you do want to avoid at any rate.
In addition the setup tables need to cleared, unless the initial loads of the past would be still there...


Hope that helps.

Regards Frank

entry.title: Re: How to find Technical User mentioned during remote so...
entry.content:
For the remote source credentials, we can tell from DP agent log, do a connection test for the remote source, search in the DP agent log for something like below example for CamelJDBCAdapter Open remote source [remote_source_name]. Parameters: {localDriverFolder=, pds_use_agent_stored_credential=false, db_credential_user=token, url=, filename=, filepath=, dbname=, port=, driverClass=, dbtype=other, host=, servername=, db_credential_password=******, remote_source_name=} Different adapter might have different log information like above. There is no way for us to tell who start the subscription. But you can run the following query to tell the owner of the subscriptions.

SELECT O.OWNER_NAME, RS.REMOTE_SOURCE_NAME, RS.SUBSCRIPTION_NAME FROM OWNERSHIP O INNER JOIN remote_subscriptions RS ON O.OBJECT_OID = RS.SUBSCRIPTION_OID WHERE RS.REMOTE_SOURCE_NAME ='Remote_source_name'

Usually for XSC, owner is _SYS_REPO, for XSA, owner is **#OO

entry.title: Re: Is Windows Server 2022 supported by SAP CC 2023
entry.content:
Hello Doris ,

for now, there is now plan for an upgrade of the SAP CC PAM regarding Windows server. 

Nevertheless, I will update you here as soon as the SAP CC product team has a commitment. 

Best regards,

Eric F.

entry.title: CIS (Cloud Identity Services) - IAS & IPS
entry.content: New to CIS (Cloud Identity Services). My understanding is that both IAS (Identity Authentication Service) and IPS (Identity Provisioning Service) are available in CIS. Is it possible to use only the IPS portion of CIS or do you have to make use of both IPS and IAS (for authentication) when using CIS?

entry.title: Re: accounts receivable and advance accounts of the same...
entry.content: 1.已将客户主数据排序键值为009，预收账款主数据排序键值是036订单，但预收不显示该账户的分摊字段，自动清算也未成功；2 .手工填写同一分摊字段时，应收账款和预收账款不能自动清算；3.应收账款和预收账款金额不等可以清算吗？

entry.title: Re: CTMS Transport fails
entry.content:
Hi @BorisZarske ,

Thank you for your reply. I followed already all the steps as mentioned but still not working.

Let me try with content Agent if this works, I will come back on this.

Regards,

Sandhya Kotian

entry.title: How to retrieve who the current approver of a work item is from top-level work item ids?
entry.content:
Hi

I have a cds view that generates a report of work items from the swwwihead and swwuserwi tables. I have already used the field user_id but it has many blanks for the approvers columns. Upon further inspection in SE11 under table SWWUSERWI, it seems that these records do not exist but are present in the SWWWIHEAD table, is this because they are top level work items? If so, how can this be used to pull the actual current approver?

define view Test_Report as select from swwwihead as x

left outer join swwwihead as z on x.wi_id = z.top_wi_id
and z.wi_type =  'W'
and z.wi_stat <> 'COMPLETED'

left outer join swwuserwi as y on z.wi_id = y.wi_id
{
x.wi_id   as WorkItemID
y.user_id as Approver
}

entry.title: Re: S2S VPN requirement?
entry.content:
Hi Daniel,

My assumption is that you're referring to RISE on Microsoft Azure. There are several scenarios available (see this blog) whereas generally besides VPN one could alternatively as well go with a dedicated Private Connection or VPC / VNET Peering.

In case your customer moves all systems to RISE and no further connectivity to the old environments is needed anymore, this route can be dismantled.

Best regards,
Tomasz

entry.title: Re: Querry Convert KPI to Characteristics
entry.content:
Hi,

i guess i found the answer:

I found a hint how this should work.

it seemded to be called  "virtual KPI" or "virtual Characteristics".

Its implemented with BADI RSR_LOAP_BADI.

The Implementation is quite lengthy:

You find it in: 
"ABAP Programming für SAP BW - User Exits und Badis" from Gallileo Press, Chapter 5.2.
In German but with extensive Codeing samples,
The booklet is only used available.

Best Martin

entry.title: Re: Sap sd
entry.content: @Vivek_Savant The requirement is that the price should be picked based on the batch. For example, if one batch is created in January and another in December for the same material, the batch with the current price (created in January) should be selected in the sales order or delivery, even if the December batch exists.

entry.title: Re: Categorize Time Valuation Counter in 3 groups using time collector
entry.content: any update?

entry.title: Re: 401 Unauthorized - Build Apps Unable to Access BTP Destination API
entry.content: Does your use have participant roles? Can you trigger the process manually via Postman? Have you made a change to the process without republishing (though I think this is a different error)?

entry.title: Re: Does sybase databse version 16.0.2.8 support SUSE Linux Enterprise Server (SLES) 15 SP5 and SP6?
entry.content: Look at the Product Availability Matrix in the SAP Support Website.
